title: Device Guidelines
content:
General Guidelines: 
 Laptop - not iPad, tablet, smartphone, or Chromebook
 Other devices welcome, but laptop will be your primary classroom device
 Wireless, webcam, and microphone built-in
 Minimum 13" display - if too large it may not fit in a backpack
 Core i5 or M1 and above
 8GB ram and 256 GB of storage minimum
 16GB ram and 512 GB storage recommended
 Battery life of 5 hours or more
 Current Mac or Windows Operating System
 Protective cover recommended
 HDMI port for connecting to displays or adapter for HDMI connection
 Ethernet port or ethernet adaptor (optional for boarders to connect with ethernet in dorm)
 Must be capable of running MS Office applications
 Students are provided with a Microsoft Office 365 license during orientation that is valid as long
as they are enrolled at Webb 
 Adobe Creative Cloud license provided to students while enrolled at Webb
 Anti-Virus software required, Sophos Home is a good option for Mac and PC
 Security patches kept up to date and set to automatically install
 Boarding student should have warranty documentation with them
 It is highly recommended to have a stable Internet connection at home to stay connected when
not on campus 
Students can bring either a Mac or PC machine.  We are a dual-platform school.
All students need adequate permissions enabled in order to install school-required 
applications on both their phone and laptop devices.
